data.title: SA analysts share their thoughts on Trump's selection of Kevin Warsh
data.description:
President Donald Trump on Friday announced the nomination of Kevin Warsh to succeed Jerome Powell as chair of the Federal Reserve, bringing to a close a prolonged and closely watched search that unfolded surrounding the central bank. The decision marks a significant turning point for U.S. monetary leadership following months of speculation and debate over the Fed’s independence.

See what Seeking Alpha analysts are saying about Trump’s nomination of Kevin Warsh and what it could mean for markets and monetary policy ahead. 

SA analyst Justin Purohit stated: “In my view, his prior experience as a Fed governor during the GFS provides him with a rare perspective on monetary policy under extreme conditions, and it is entirely possible that, like Powell, Warsh ultimately emerges as a pragmatic, straight-shooting central banker rather than the political instrument many fear,” in Kevin Warsh Nominated To Be Next Fed Chair, What It Means For Markets. 

SA analyst Michael Gray noted: “While Warsh is experienced and has served previously as a Fed Governor, he will have a challenging task in asserting his leadership as Fed Chair. One obstacle is that he will have to prove his independence, when a litmus test for being nominated as Fed Chair was agreeing with President Trump that interest rates should be much lower,” in The Changing Of The Guard At The Fed Begins. 

SA analyst Multiplo Invest indicated: “Warsh advocates a smaller balance sheet and interest rate cuts, which appears to have attracted President Trump to choose him. Total assets under Fed management currently stand at $6.5 trillion. The value reached a record high of nearly $9 trillion in 2022, as the Fed began the process of shrinking its asset balance sheet,” in My Hot Take On Kevin Warsh's Nomination. 

SA analyst Hawkinvest said: “While I believe the new Fed chair will work to lower interest rates, I think expectations might be too high in terms of where interest rates are likely to go in 2026 and beyond,” in Trump's New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh: The Impact And Rate Path For 2026. 

Market Index ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

Fixed Income ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), (BIL), (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), (VTIP), (TIP), (SCHP), (STIP), (TIPX), (SPIP), (WIP), (GTIP), (LQDI), and (RINF).

data.title: Wall Street's decline continues as tech trade sell-off deepens; Warsh picked as new Fed chair
data.description:
Wall Street's main averages were lower on Friday as investors continued to assess the latest company earnings and technology stocks carried on declining.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.2% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

Both the S&P 500 ((SP500)) and the Nasdaq ((COMP:IND)) are—so far—on track for weekly gains, while the Dow ((DJI)) might take home losses for the week. However, all three indexes are set for monthly gains in January.

“Bulls have reason to smile: a positive January often sets the tone for a bullish year. When the S&P 500 (SP500) finishes the month higher, history shows full-year gains averaging 16.9% since 1950,” said Ryan Detrick, chief market strategist at Carson Group.

Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and silver (XAGUSD:CUR) prices continued to lower, now with the precious metals back around the $5,000/oz and $100/oz levels, respectively.

Investors turned their focus on U.S. President Donald Trump's selection for Kevin Warsh as the Federal Reserve chair.

“I have a tough time seeing Kevin Warsh being able to persuade his colleagues to a dovish position, a position that elevates employment risks over inflation and one he has never been able to make himself, even when the times demanded,” said Neil Dutta, head of economic research at Renaissance Macro.

“Markets tend to test new Fed chairs. The average correction in the first six months is 15%. When the chair faces their first crisis, investors don't wait and see how they handle it. Fed independence is a potential crisis for Warsh, but it could be anything,” said Ed Clissold, chief U.S. strategist for Ned Davis Research.

In other economic news, the producer price index came in hotter than expected in December.

On earnings, Apple (AAPL) released its first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results that were driven by record-breaking iPhone sales. Shares were last -0.5%.

Also, Visa's (V) fiscal Q1 2026 earnings and revenue topped the average analyst estimates, but their stock was last -2%. KLA (KLAC) shares also dipped -10.6% even after its Q2 results topped estimates.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.24%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was 2 basis points lower at 3.55%.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: VZ, SNDK, TSLA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Friday, the last trading day of January, as investors weighed reports of Kevin Warsh potentially replacing Jerome Powell as Federal Reserve chairman, while gold and silver pulled back from record highs.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • SanDisk (SNDK) +19% - Shares jumped after the company delivered a blowout FQ2, with revenue up 61% Y/Y driven by a sharp acceleration in data center demand. Data center revenue surged 64% Q/Q to $440M, while edge and consumer revenues rose 21% and 39%, respectively. The upside extended to guidance. Q3 adjusted EPS was seen at $12–$14, far above the $4.41 consensus, and revenue was guided to $4.4B–$4.8B. Sentiment was further boosted after SanDisk and Kioxia extended their Yokkaichi joint venture by five years to 2034.
  • Deckers Outdoor (DECK) +12% - Shares jumped after the company posted record FQ3 profit and revenue, driven by strong demand for UGG and HOKA across both wholesale and DTC channels. HOKA sales rose 18.5% to $628.9M, and UGG sales increased 4.9% to $1.31B, lifting total revenue 7.1% to $1.96B, well above expectations. Adjusted EPS climbed 11% to $3.33, beating estimates despite a 50 bp margin contraction. On the back of the strong quarter, Deckers raised its full-year outlook, guiding EPS of $6.80–$6.85 and higher sales, margins, and brand growth assumptions, all above Street expectations.
  • Lumentum (LITE) +10% - Shares advanced ahead of its FQ2 results next week after Morgan Stanley raised its price target to $350 from $304, citing stronger margin gains from price hikes, optical circuit switching opportunities, and exposure to Google. The bank kept an Equal-weight rating, noting the stock has already doubled since November and expectations for longer-term earnings have moved sharply higher, tempering near-term upside as capacity constraints remain a concern. The company reports Tuesday, with consensus looking for $1.40 in adjusted EPS on $652M in revenue.
  • Verizon (VZ) +7% - Shares rose after the telecom major delivered a strong Q4 beat and issued an upbeat full-year outlook, supported by robust postpaid phone additions that comfortably topped expectations. The company added 616,000 postpaid phone subscribers in Q4, well ahead of consensus, and guided to 750,000–1M net additions in 2026. Verizon also forecast adjusted EPS of $4.90–$4.95, above estimates, with steady service revenue and solid free cash flow outlook, helping offset investor caution around largely flat wireless service growth and slightly lower capex plans. The company raised quarterly dividend by 2.5% and announced up to $25B share repurchase program. Expects to buy back at least $3B of shares in 2026.
  • Tesla (TSLA) +2% - Shares rose after Bloomberg reported SpaceX is considering a merger with Tesla or an alternative combination with xAI, potentially exchanging xAI shares for SpaceX equity ahead of a possible mid-2026 IPO. The move aligns with SpaceX's plans for orbital data centers powered by Starlink to bolster xAI's AI infrastructure, creating synergies across Elon Musk's ventures. This speculation followed Tesla's recent disclosure of a roughly $2B investment in xAI to advance AI initiatives under Master Plan Part IV, fueling investor enthusiasm for Musk's interconnected ecosystem despite the talks remaining preliminary.

Biggest stock losers

  • PennyMac Financial Services (PFSI) -35% - Shares plunged after the mortgage lender missed Q4 earnings estimates, with investors rattled by margin compression from rising industry capacity, tougher competition in origination, fast prepayments, and higher FHA delinquencies. Management acknowledged near-term pressure but pointed to efficiency gains from its new Vesta loan origination system, which is cutting processing time and costs. It said production income should improve in early 2026 as volumes shift toward higher-margin direct channels, though analysts remain cautious on margin durability and excess capacity risks.
  • Schneider National (SNDR) -8% - Shares plunged after Q4 earnings missed expectations, with weakness in the intermodal segment weighing on results. Management said results came in below guidance due to softer demand from November, a shortened peak season, higher third-party carrier costs, auto production shutdowns, and elevated healthcare expenses. While conditions improved late in December, it wasn’t enough to offset earlier pressures. For 2026, Schneider guided a full-year EPS of $0.70–$1.00, well below the $1.07 consensus.

  • KLA (KLAC) -8% - Shares tumbled despite FQ2 beating earnings and revenue, as the market focused on softer free cash flow vs. estimates and a muted reaction to guidance. Adjusted EPS came in at $8.85 on revenue of $3.3B, both slightly ahead of consensus, with strength in services offsetting modest product growth. While Q3 guidance topped expectations, investors appeared cautious on near-term momentum despite management highlighting strong positioning in AI-driven demand across foundry, memory, and advanced packaging.

data.title: KLA falls amid WFE outlook concerns despite bullish stance from analysts
data.description:
Shares of KLA (KLAC) fell about 9% on Friday after investor concerns over the company's wafer fab equipment, or WFE, outlook despite analysts remaining largely bullish and raising price targets.

KeyBanc Capital kept its Sector Weight rating on the chip equipment maker's stock. 

"KLA shares were down AH following F1Q results and guide slightly ahead of Street. We think investors likely have consternation with KLA’s WFE guide of +HSD-LDD [high single digit-low double digit] vs. LRCX’s +23%, but both landed at a total CY26 figure of ~$135B. Similarly, however, KLA indicated a 2H weighting with 2H26 growth accelerating from +MSD h/h in 1H26. It’s also seeing some margin drag from higher cost DRAM components and tariffs," said analysts led by Steve Barger.  

The analysts noted that they ultimately view KLA as positioned to succeed in what should be a robust market, but understand investor anxiety with high expectations for expensively valued shares.

J.P. Morgan maintained its Overweight rating and increased the price target on the stock to $1950 from $1485.

"KLA delivered strong Dec-Q results (revenues/margins/EPS) on solid Q/Q trends in its core process control and its services business, as demand for advanced-node technologies from its foundry/logic and memory customers (DRAM/HBM) continues to strengthen, combined with strong growth in advanced packaging. For the March quarter outlook, the team guided revenues/EPS above consensus expectations, as the advanced node ramp remains strong—supported by significant leverage to TSMC’s N3/N2 and advanced node DRAM suppliers— along with continued momentum in advanced packaging," said analysts led by Harlan Sur.

However, the analysts added that revenue upside is currently limited by supply constraints (1H of calendar year) as KLA is sold out through the first half of the year, though the company expects to unlock more supply as the year progresses. 

Demand is constrained by both KLA tool availability and customer fab readiness, which should extend growth into 2027, according to the analysts.

The analysts noted that KLA expects 2026 WFE spending to grow low double-digit percentages, reaching mid-$130B (including $12B from advanced packaging view), building off an around $121B WFE base ($110B core WFE and $11B advanced packaging).

"The 2026 WFE outlook is similar to peers at mid-$130B, but the Y/Y rate is slightly lower, which could be due to different base assumptions and the characterization of core WFE versus advanced packaging. China revenue should be more resilient in CY26 than previously anticipated, at mid- to high-20% range, with a flattish to slightly positive China WFE spending environment. Given supply constraints, customer fab readiness, and visibility into new greenfield projects, the team anticipates WFE growth to extend into 2027," said Sur and his team.

The analysts noted that KLA remains confident it will continue to outgrow WFE, given a spending environment expected to favor advanced node foundry/logic (with ongoing 3 nm capacity builds and the 2 nm ramp), expanding advanced DRAM spending, and rising process control intensitydue to higher manufacturing complexity.

Wells Fargo kept its Overweight rating on KLA and raised the price target on the stock to $1,900 from $1,600.

"KLAC is down AMC due to confusion on its 2026 WFE growth est [estimates] relative to Lam. While elevated val required a clean qtr/guide, we think the neg [negative] share reaction is misplaced as ests increase again. Buy on weakness," said analyst Joe Quatrochi and his team.

BofA reiterated its Buy rating and increased the price target on the shares to $1,850 from $1,650.

"KLAC’s core WFE CY26 guide of low-$120bn (+11% YoY) excluding $12bn in advanced packaging is lower than LRCX’s $130bn (+18% YoY) core WFE (excl. ~$5bn packaging) which along with slower revenue growth (LRCX signaled >23% YoY), is likely weighing on shares post print. We suspect there is fear KLAC is losing share given WFE mix shift towards memory (KLAC’s F/L sales growing +15% YoY vs. memory at +43%) which led to stock underperformance during CY12-28," said analysts led by Vivek Arya.

Shares of other chip equipment makers on Thursday. ASML (ASML) and Lam Research (LRCX) were largely flat but in the green, while Applied Materials (AMAT) dipped nearly 1%.

data.title: Panama's top court strikes down CK Hutchison port contracts in blow to China
data.description:
Panama's Supreme Court ruled late Thursday it annulled key contracts held by Hong Kong-based CK Hutchison (CKHUY) (CKHUF) to operate two ports on the Panama Canal, leaving the future ownership of some Canal operations unclear.

The court said the terms under which CK Hutchison (CKHUY) (CKHUF) runs the ports of Balboa on the Pacific coast and Cristobal on the Atlantic coast were unconstitutional, setting the stage for the company's departure from its container terminals.

Panama Ports Company, a CK Hutchison (CKHUY) (CKHUF) subsidiary, has held contracts since the 1990s to operate the port facilities, separate from the waterway's operations.

The decision could disrupt CK Hutchison's (CKHUY) (CKHUF) proposed $23B sale of dozens of ports worldwide, including the Panamanian terminals, to a consortium led by BlackRock (BLK) and Mediterranean Shipping Company.

Once the license is revoked, the government aims to ensure the continuity of port operations by hiring A.P. Moeller-Maersk (AMKBY) (AMKBF) to manage the facilities until it opens a bidding process with new license terms, Panama President Mulino said.

The decision had been closely watched by the U.S. and China, and is viewed as a key step in their broader dispute over the Panama Canal; at least 5% of global trade flows through the Panama Canal.

CK Hutchison (CKHUY) (CKHUF) shares fell 4.7% in Hong Kong trading Friday.

data.title: Why Fed Governor Waller thinks more rate cuts are needed
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said on Friday that he favored a 25-basis-point rate cut at the Federal Open Market Committee's meeting on Wednesday because he expects that the labor market is much weaker than the data is showing.

He was one of two Fed governors to dissent from the decision to keep the federal funds rate target range at 3.50%-3.75%. Stephen Miran is the other who voted in favor of cutting the rate by 25 bps.

Nonfarm payroll growth data indicate that 600K jobs were created in 2025, down from the previous 10-year average of ~1.9M, he said in a statement.  "And, last year's data will be revised downward soon to likely show that there was virtually no growth in payroll employment in 2025," he said. "Zero. Zip. Nada."

"This does not remotely look like a healthy labor market," he added. While lower labor supply is a factor, Waller also sees “considerable weakness” in labor demand.

On the inflation side of the Fed's mandate, it's appropriate to “look through” the effects of tariffs on inflation as long as inflation expectations are anchored, "which they are." Excluding the tariff effects on inflation, due to their one-time nature, inflation is running close to the Fed's 2% target "and on a path to sustainably reach that goal," he explained.

"Three cuts to the policy rate last year have moved it closer to a neutral setting, but monetary policy is still restricting economic activity, and economic data make it clear to me further easing is needed," he said.

For those reasons, "the policy rate should be closer to neutral, which the median FOMC participant estimates is 3%, and not where we are — 50 to 75 basis points above 3%," Waller said. "I favored reducing the policy rate to strengthen the labor market and guard against a deterioration that would be harder to address once it has begun."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Ameriprise Financial upgraded at Piper Sandler after Q4 earnings
data.description:
Ameriprise Financial (AMP) was upgraded at Piper Sandler on the back of stability in the margins of the financial services provider's largest business – Advice & Wealth Management.

The stock was raised to Neutral from Underweight, and the price target was increased to $530.00 from $434.00. Shares were +1.24% to $527.82 on Friday after the opening bell.

The Minneapolis-based company reported Q4 non-GAAP EPS of $10.83, which beats by $0.52, and revenue of $5.05B (+8.6% Y/Y), which beats by $310M.

"Many headwinds present since 3Q24 leading to share price underperformance on a relative basis appear to be somewhat receding," said analyst John Barnidge.

"Results demonstrated the moat built is underappreciated by both us and investors," said the research note.

Piper Sandler's rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating of Hold. On the other hand, SA authors grade AMP as Buy.

data.title: Chicago PMI rises far more than expected in January
data.description: The Chicago PMI surged to 54.0 in January from 42.7 in the prior month, which was revised from 43.5, exceeding the 43.5 consensus, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Friday.

data.title: Red River Bancshares GAAP EPS of $1.73, revenue of $33.19M
data.description:
• Red River Bancshares press release (RRBI): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.73.
  • Revenue of $33.19M.
  • As of December 31, 2025, loans held for investment (“HFI”) were $2.25 billion, up $75.6 million, or 3.5%, from $2.17 billion as of September 30, 2025. In the fourth quarter of 2025, we experienced robust new loan and commitment activity, combined with funding of loan construction commitments.
  • As of December 31, 2025, assets were $3.35 billion, up $136.5 million, or 4.2%, from $3.21 billion as of September 30, 2025, driven by a $124.6 million increase in deposits.
  • Deposits totaled $2.96 billion as of December 31, 2025, up $124.6 million, or 4.4%, from $2.84 billion as of September 30, 2025, primarily due to the seasonal inflow of funds from public entity customers combined with higher customer deposit balances.
  • We paid a quarterly cash dividend of $0.15 per common share in the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • In 2025, our cash dividend was $0.54 per common share, which was a 50.0% increase from $0.36 per common share paid in 2024.
  • The 2025 stock repurchase program authorized us to purchase up to $5.0 million of our outstanding shares of common stock from January 1, 2025 through December 31, 2025. We repurchased shares on the open market during the second quarter of 2025, when we repurchased 11,748 shares at an aggregate cost of $656,000, excluding excise tax. The 2025 stock repurchase program expired on December 31, 2025, with $4.3 million of available capacity.
  •
